rulings.Case Title: MARY DEBORAH AVEGGIO ET AL VS. ADVANCE AUTO PARTS, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 7, 2018 in Department 503, Line 1. Detroit Stoker Company, LLC's motion to quash for lack of personal jurisdiction is continued on the court's own motion to May 2, 2018, based on the court's understanding that issues regarding jurisdictional discovery have not yet been resolved. The briefing schedule is amended as follows: supplemental opposition, if any, due by April 19, 2018. Supplemental reply, if any, due by April 25, 2018. If a hearing is requested to contest this continuance, it will be at 9:30a.m., Hon. Lynn O'Malley-Taylor presiding. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. =(503/LOT)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH COONS ET AL VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion And Motion To Appoint David Coons As Successor-In-Interest To, And Substitute Successor-In-Interest For, Deceased Plaintiff And For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 7, 2018 in Department 503, Line 2. Plaintiffs and Petitioner's motion to appoint David Coons as successor-in-interest to, and substitute successor-in-interest for, deceased plaintiff and for leave to file first amended complaint is granted. The First Amended Complaint is deemed served as of the date of granting this motion on all defendants who have previously appeared. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m., Hon. Lynn O'Malley-Taylor presiding. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/LOT)
